item.title: I think I found some footage of 1316. Decoding the Morse in this clip read “All Shall Perish”
item.content:encoded: https://youtu.be/XOlVZiEtvTE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly the twist of this being a Chaos Insurgency spy combined with the 'cute kitten vote for me' element of this scip really made me dislike this. If this were an ageless kitten transmitting to some unknown location I would probably have upvoted but as it stands it's a -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How to tell if your cat is plotting to kill you:
When your cat is a Chaos Insurgency mole.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey Gaff. Don't think anything else can be said positively for this article.

I have some questions though. If this thing has stopped transmitting, shouldn't it be dropped to Safe? I feel like the "radio frequency shielding material" would be enough to counteract its primary function (or a Faraday-cage would take care of it if not). Aside from 'immortal kitty', it doesn't seem like this thing is very anomalous anymore.

Are we operating under the assumption that it's still being utilized as a probe/spy in some ways? It seems like The Chaos Insurgency has no intention of retrieving SCP-1316, and we seem to know how to keep in under wraps. So shouldn't this be brought down to Safe? (I'd suggest Neutralized if kitty was mortal). Thanks for your time Gaffney. Good stuff obviously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I did always wonder if it was a good idea for a lot of the SCPs (especially humanoid ones) to be able to walk around, even with escorts. This SCP just made me even more suspicious of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Im just imagining somebody yelling at a kitten while it sits derpily with a vacant expression of itself

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't believe no one's connected this to Acoustic Kitty yet. I assumed this was a direct reference to it. Upvoted because I love Acoustic Kitty.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Acoustic_Kitty

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was pointed out to me that I was being a doof, to say nothing of, apparently, a real tool about things relating to this article. Particularly, that the kitty picture I was using was under a non-CC-compatible license. So I replaced the old copyleft kitty with a new copyleft kitty.

New kitty spy is taken from Wikimedia commons and is by user Sasan Geranmehr. It is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported license.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Hannibus42, please only let the article influence your vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would've upvoted… but I saw you being a real tool to that other guy… so, no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my god, I'm going to cry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wonder how CI recuited it, clearly it is just an amateur spy.

Else it should be aware that any hope for rescue is gone the moment it pretty much tell them "I got caught".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a soft spot for bad guys trapped and isolated from their groups. I'd feel bad for him/her even if he/she was just a regular human. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know we have two other "CI plants thing in Foundation" articles, but what made this particularly good is the fact that it's fundamentally a very simple tool that gets treated exactly like that: it served its purpose and was "disposed." It's simple and it works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why not just change the containment requirements to put the kitten in a faraday cage?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved this until the inclusion of the CI in it. The fact that it was basically a spy for a group of normal humans just killed the entire mystery and "anomalous" nature for me. I get that anomalies are rather like magic, in that they can just be sufficiently advanced technology, but the closer/more realistic that technology seems to be to actual human potential (give or take a few centuries), the less interesting an anomaly becomes for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if ALL cats work for the Chaos Insurgency?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a kitten spy!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this so much, mostly for the sad agent-kitten but also because I view the continued containment of 1316 as a direct middle-finger at the CI. And likewise, the idea of using an anomalous and 'dangerous' item to one-up the Foundation in the first place almost seems like CI trying to teach the Foundation a lesson in how dangerous and treacherous containing these things is.

Or maybe I'm just reading too much into it…

<3 upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am a huge fan of the current picture of the kitten climbing the cage bars for reasons that have nothing to do with the SCP. Also, I am a huge fan of this SCP.

(we went to adopt a kitten and I saw one that was climbing the cage bars and being a total brat and I just knew it was the one that Shannon would end up wanting and yeah sure enough we have that kitten now)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: (Insert link to a TF2 character's "Spy!" Voice command here)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really wish I had the skill to write a tale myself. As it stands this is the saddest thing I've read on the site.

I can imagine a tale dealing with 1316's interrogations and tribulations adapting to a new life within the foundation. In the end she'd be curled up with Josie, worse for wear but overall all right.

If anyone wants to write this story, please do!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for being an abandoned spy, just the implied resignation of its last transmission felt poignant to me.

item.title: Admin Post
item.content:encoded:
Kinne, you are entitled to your opinion, but you are not entitled to be nasty to other users because of it. Posts like this one, which is content-free except for personal attacks, are unacceptable.

This is your official warning.

(edit) Whoops, Moose sniped me. This is to be taken as concurrence with the above warning, not a second separate one.

item.title: Admin Post
item.content:encoded:
SCP isn't a creepypasta website. This is for interesting things. So you found the wrong website. Just google creepypast and leave us to the intelligent fictions.

Further posts like this will get you banned. Consider this your official warning. One was already on its way, but you're shitposting too fast to wait for a proper official PM.

This line of discussion is now closed. PM me or another member of staff with any questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok, now you're just shitposting. All Reject said was that he preferred horror, and that's no reason to criticize him for it and telling him to read creepypasta.
You're necro-posting a ton, and none of it is actually contributing to the article's discussion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP isn't a creepypasta website. This is for interesting things. So you found the wrong website. Just google creepypast and leave us to the intelligent fictions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's assuming that they let the cat/agent know anything worthwhile before setting her out. Which they didn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Indeed. Also kind of stupid of them if the cat decides to play double agent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fact that they left the cat and aren't going to extract it, so sad…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP articles are snapshots in time and do not necessarily contain all documentation pertaining to an object. You're welcome to write a tale about it, however.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, as it's a good piece, but I feel as if there's a chunk missing. It's a captured spy, which by this time likely has decreased loyalty to its original masters. It's also clearly intelligent and capable of communicating. As such, shouldn't there at least be a mention of an interrogation/interview? (even if not explicitly written out).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Minimal security measures are fine, particularly for Safe objects. Not everything has to be guarded by a quadruple-blind task force of Benedictine monks or whatever; hell, I've written stuff where the containment procedures are "put it in a locker." This was a sort of commentary of 131 and 529 where the containment procedures are essentially "let them go wherever they want (because they're cute)."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a brilliant example of why we contain things ,as opposed to having them run around

This removes a lot of my temptations to make minimal security measures. Fantastic precedent set, so now I'm obligated to lock safe items up a bit tighter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Changed the picture to something with a less… questionable copyright. I'm not a huge fan of the new picture, but it does the job. If people have suggestions for CC pictures of kittens that look better, I'm all ears (eyes?).

sauce: http://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Felis_silvestris_catus_%28kitten_in_cage%29.jpg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read and upvoted this articles ages ago, apparently, looked at it again and I want to upvote more.
It's a brilliant example of why we contain things ,as opposed to having them run around, no matter how cute they are.

Also, to the people who claim the Foundation would destroy this - It's a kitten that apparently neither ages or dies, which is a property we can't replicate so far. It'd almost certainly be studied. Poor kitty.

item.title: Horror, oh horror
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted this so hard, for the sheer horror of being scp-1316.

Just imagine it: You are in a form that can't hurt anyone, you can't even commit suicide. People you hate the most in the whole world are taking care of you, insuring that you don't die. And every single day, for years and years, decades and decades, some stranger would enter your personal space, give you food and say "Who's a little kitty? You are! Yes you are!", petting you without your permission, mocking you with every word! Forever, since you don't age!

Horrifying. I doubt that after 60+ years without a single emission poor guy even knows how to send radio signals any more.

Anyway, +1. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't love it.

item.title: Only a month and a half late, but...
item.content:encoded: This was a response to one of Bright's challenges. I rolled up a cat what gives mysterious information.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! Also, fix'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved it. Went from "daaww" to "oh…" to "…oh…". Have an upvote.

One minor thing, and it may have already been pointed out, but you say it's been in Foundation custody since 1958 in the description, but the old report says it was acquired in 1948. Just thought I'd point that out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Following standard counterintelligence practice, all Foundation personnel were debriefed and a scan of the site for bugs ensued.

I would think that Foundation sites would be regularly swept for bugs, rather than just doing it after security breaches.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That or the lady was the associated with the CI/threatened with death unless she claimed to have the kitten for decades. For all the Foundation knows, she might have gotten the kitten two days before she told the newspaper about it. Or maybe Researcher Skippy had some really bad gambling debts.

As for the consciousness thing, it seems to be kind of a sticking point, so I'm going to leave it alone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, wait. The CI implanted an agent's consciousness inside a kitten (or whatever), and then had the kitten live with an old lady for ten years on the off chance that she'd brag about her immortal kitten in a forum where the Foundation could hear about it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If this was meant to evoke pity, it worked. I'll help extract you kitty, then we can play together forever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I suppose, but given that a) the Foundation has blocked 1316's ability to communicate (that we know of, yes, but there were other tests run that indicate no other weirdness) b) 1316 doesn't know anything about where it is outside of "it's a room with a cage" c) Bio Containment Site 39 is a cinder block building with maybe two researchers, a D-Class, two guards, and some pet supplies the chances of the CI pulling off a successful raid are very low.

The only reason that the CI used 1316 in the first place was to get the artifacts stored at Site 112, not to fuck with the Foundation because lulz.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still, even if there aren't other objects at that site, the CI could spend a lot less money to destroy/steal shit from it than the Foundation spent making the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In the end, we are all Skippy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That worked!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I couldn't see the Foundation allowing an ageless warthog free run of a site. There's an institutional bias towards letting cute things wander.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like I said, punishment detail. Also, it's not just one guy. There're probably legions of junior researchers and assistants whose job it is to investigate "weird but not dangerous" crap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They're not keeping it because it's cute, they're keeping it because it's an anomaly and it's against Foundation policy to destroy anomalous objects unless absolutely necessary.

They've minimized risk by having be the only object stored at the site and by only allowing D-Class to interact with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kitten doesn't equal cute per se.. Look at the picture, this kitten is looking at me and I get the idea it's saying "what the f*#k did you just call me?"
Anyway, I like this. So +1, but that kitten ain't cute. I gueass that's what this site does to you :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't "death the author", I just don't, or judging from the number of upvotes, can't appreciate this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get it that it's the anomalous part, but the fact that it just too much reminds me of a simple spy robot, even though it's not, still stands.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh. I actually like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This isn't actually unreasonable. Sure, the Foundation likes to keep anomalous objects around, but it won't do so to the extent that it might compromise its security. This thing works for the CI, and for all we know still may. It's a pretty big risk to let a spy remain, even if it is an anomalous one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think you can, it's the concept. The foundation would destroy such a device even if it was subjectively 'cute'. It just doesn't work. Having it around is a liability.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I'm seeing a lot of veering away from scary. This isn't inherently a bad thing, but I like scary better. A lot better. It's why I came to this site. And this SCP reads to me like 1048 minus the scary.
